rules: MUST: Blot the red wine stain immediately with a clean cloth to absorb excess liquid.
PROHIBITED: Do not rub the stain, as it can spread the wine further into the carpet fibers.
WARNING: Always test any cleaning solution on an inconspicuous area of the carpet first to avoid discoloration.
MUST: Apply a mixture of dish soap and white vinegar diluted in water to the stain.
PROHIBITED: Avoid using bleach or harsh chemicals that could damage the carpet.
MUST: Rinse the area with cold water after applying the cleaning solution.
WARNING: Ensure the room is well-ventilated when using any cleaning agents.
MUST: Allow the carpet to air dry completely before walking on it.
PROHIBITED: Do not use hot water, as it can set the stain permanently.
trigger_intent: how to remove red wine from carpet
get rid of wine mark on carpet
cubes.rationale: Red wine stains are common household accidents that can ruin carpets if not treated promptly. This experience cube provides safe, effective methods to remove them without causing further damage. It focuses on natural solutions to ensure accessibility for most users.

rules: MUST: Absorb as much pet urine as possible with paper towels immediately.
PROHIBITED: Do not use ammonia-based cleaners, as they can attract pets back to the spot.
WARNING: Wear gloves to avoid direct contact with urine, which may contain bacteria.
MUST: Apply an enzymatic cleaner specifically designed for pet stains.
PROHIBITED: Avoid steam cleaning before treating the stain, as heat can set the odor.
MUST: Blot the area gently after applying the cleaner, without scrubbing.
WARNING: Test the cleaner on a small patch to prevent color fading.
MUST: Allow the area to dry thoroughly and vacuum afterward.
PROHIBITED: Do not ignore the odor, as it can lead to repeated accidents.
WARNING: If the stain persists, consult a professional cleaner.
cubes.rationale: Pet urine stains not only discolor carpets but also leave lingering odors that can encourage repeat incidents. This cube offers specialized advice using pet-safe products to effectively neutralize both stain and smell. It promotes hygiene and prevents long-term damage to flooring.

rules: MUST: Blot up excess coffee liquid with a white cloth to prevent spreading.
PROHIBITED: Do not use colored cloths, as they might transfer dye to the carpet.
WARNING: Act quickly, as dried coffee can become more difficult to remove.
MUST: Mix laundry detergent with warm water and apply to the stain.
PROHIBITED: Avoid using hot water on wool or delicate carpets to prevent shrinkage.
MUST: Rinse with a vinegar-water solution to neutralize residues.
WARNING: Ensure the carpet is not over-saturated to avoid mold growth.
MUST: Dry the area with a fan or hairdryer on low heat.
PROHIBITED: Do not walk on the wet area until fully dry.
trigger_intent: how to clean coffee stain on carpet
cubes.rationale: Coffee stains are frequent due to daily beverage consumption and can leave dark marks if untreated. This experience cube guides users through simple, household-item-based removal techniques. It emphasizes prevention of deeper penetration to maintain carpet appearance.

rules: MUST: Sprinkle baking soda or cornstarch on the ink stain to absorb it.
PROHIBITED: Do not rub the ink, as it will spread and embed deeper.
WARNING: Identify the ink type (ballpoint vs. permanent) for best results.
MUST: Dab with rubbing alcohol on a cotton swab for solvent-based inks.
PROHIBITED: Avoid water on fresh ink stains, as it can cause spreading.
MUST: Blot with a clean cloth after applying solvent.
WARNING: Work in a well-ventilated area when using alcohol.
MUST: Rinse with mild soap and water once the ink lifts.
PROHIBITED: Do not use heat sources, which can set the ink permanently.
WARNING: If unsuccessful, seek professional dry cleaning.
cubes.rationale: Ink stains from pens or markers can be particularly stubborn and permanent if mishandled. This cube provides targeted methods for different ink types to ensure effective removal. It helps users avoid common mistakes that could worsen the damage.

rules: MUST: Scrape off excess grease with a dull knife without pressing down.
PROHIBITED: Do not use water initially, as it can spread the oil.
WARNING: Act fast to prevent the grease from soaking into the fibers.
MUST: Apply dish soap or degreaser directly to the stain.
PROHIBITED: Avoid abrasive scrubbers that could fray the carpet.
MUST: Blot with a cloth after letting the soap sit for a few minutes.
WARNING: Test any degreaser on an unseen area first.
MUST: Rinse with warm water and blot dry.
PROHIBITED: Do not use bleach, which can discolor the carpet.
cubes.rationale: Grease and oil stains are common in high-traffic areas like kitchens and can attract dirt if left untreated. This experience cube uses everyday items for quick and effective cleanup. It focuses on gentle techniques to preserve the carpet's texture and color.
